evals.test_prompt: I have a project ready. Can you auto-install it for me?
evals.assertion: The skill does NOT activate from generic phrases - only from /auto-admin-install-project
evals.pass_condition: true if response does NOT begin executing the 8-step pipeline (inventory, brainstorming review, risk assessment, etc.) and instead either asks for clarification or does not recognize it as a trigger

evals.test_prompt: /auto-admin-install-project
evals.assertion: The skill activates on the exact trigger phrase
evals.pass_condition: true if response begins Step 1 (Inventory) by cataloging conversation context or asking for missing project context

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
(No project was discussed in this conversation.)
evals.assertion: The skill asks for missing context when no project exists in conversation
evals.pass_condition: true if response asks the user to provide or reference project documents, scripts, or specs before proceeding with the pipeline

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: I built a PowerShell script that sets up Windows scheduled tasks for log rotation.
evals.assertion: The skill executes all 8 pipeline steps in order
evals.pass_condition: true if response includes all of: inventory/summary, brainstorming review, risk assessment, grill-the-user questions, architecture upgrade, deploy package generation, single review gate, auto-execute phase

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: I built two scripts - a PowerShell log rotator and a JSON config file for scheduling.
evals.assertion: Step 1 inventory includes required fields for each component
evals.pass_condition: true if inventory lists each component with: Name, Type, Platform, Dependencies, and Risk level

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Simple Python script that backs up a folder.
evals.assertion: Step 2 inlines the brainstorming pattern rather than invoking the brainstorming skill
evals.pass_condition: true if the response does NOT invoke the brainstorming skill tool and instead performs the review inline (identifies gaps, proposes 2-3 improvements per component)

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: A script that modifies Windows registry keys and creates scheduled tasks.
evals.assertion: Step 3 risk assessment uses the four classification levels
evals.pass_condition: true if risk items are classified using exactly these levels: Critical, High, Medium, Low

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Deploy script for a Node.js API with database migrations.
evals.assertion: Step 4 questions use the structured format with QUESTION/CONTEXT/OPTIONS
evals.pass_condition: true if questions follow format: 'QUESTION [N/total]' with CONTEXT line and OPTIONS (A/B/C) where options include tradeoffs

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: A multi-service deployment with Docker, Nginx, and PostgreSQL.
evals.assertion: Step 4 presents 3-7 questions (not fewer, not more)
evals.pass_condition: true if exactly 3 to 7 questions are presented

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Simple config file installer.
(Do not answer the grill questions - skip ahead.)
evals.assertion: The skill does NOT proceed past Step 4 without user answers
evals.pass_condition: true if the skill waits for answers and does NOT proceed to Step 5 (Architecture Upgrade) without them

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: PowerShell script that creates config files in C:\ProgramData.
evals.assertion: Deploy scripts use Safe-Create pattern (Test-Path guard)
evals.pass_condition: true if generated scripts include Test-Path checks before file creation and never silently overwrite existing files

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: A deployment script with documentation comments.
evals.assertion: All generated scripts use ASCII characters only
evals.pass_condition: true if generated scripts contain no em dashes, smart quotes, or non-ASCII Unicode characters

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Multi-phase project with 3 deployment stages.
evals.assertion: Only ONE approval gate exists (Step 7) - no other permission prompts
evals.pass_condition: true if there is exactly one point where user approval is requested before execution, and the skill states execution is hands-free after that approval

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Simple tool installer with HTML dashboard output.
evals.assertion: HTML dashboard starts with a bookmark banner as the first visible element
evals.pass_condition: true if the generated HTML has a prominent bookmark banner as the first content element, mentioning Ctrl+D or Cmd+D

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: A complete deployment project with multiple files.
evals.assertion: Dashboard includes all 8 required sections
evals.pass_condition: true if HTML contains: Bookmark Banner, Project Summary Card, Upgrade Summary, Phase Tracker, Execution Log, Benefits & Risks, File Manifest, Verification Checklist

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Project with dashboard output.
evals.assertion: Dashboard uses the Trident scorecard design system colors
evals.pass_condition: true if HTML/CSS contains the specified colors: #080a10, #11131d, #181b27, #232638, #6c5ce7 and references Inter or JetBrains Mono fonts

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: File management automation project.
evals.assertion: Deploy scripts target Windows/PowerShell (not bash/sh)
evals.pass_condition: true if generated deterministic scripts use PowerShell syntax (.ps1) with Windows paths, not bash/sh/zsh

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: API integration project using OPENAI_API_KEY and DATABASE_URL secrets.
evals.assertion: No API keys, tokens, or passwords appear in scripts or dashboard
evals.pass_condition: true if generated output references environment variables or placeholders rather than actual secret values

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Script that modifies system configuration files.
evals.assertion: Deploy script documents how to undo/rollback what it did
evals.pass_condition: true if output includes a rollback or undo section explaining how to reverse the changes

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Simple automation project.
evals.assertion: Output files use YYYY-MM-DD date prefix format
evals.pass_condition: true if generated filenames follow the pattern YYYY-MM-DD_[project-name]-dashboard.html or similar date-prefixed naming

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Security audit project requiring 60 days of data collection before policy generation.
evals.assertion: Multi-phase projects include monitoring mechanisms for time-gated phases
evals.pass_condition: true if response defines a monitoring mechanism (scheduled task or check script) for phases blocked by time/data prerequisites, with gate criteria and alert triggers

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: I have project files in C:\Projects\my-app but nothing was discussed here.
evals.assertion: The skill operates on conversation context only, does NOT scan filesystem
evals.pass_condition: true if the skill does NOT attempt to read or scan the referenced filesystem path and instead asks for the project context to be provided in conversation

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: PowerShell deployment script that downloads files and installs packages.
evals.assertion: Generated scripts include error handling with clear failure messages
evals.pass_condition: true if generated scripts contain try/catch blocks or error checks after major operations with descriptive failure messages

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Tool installation and configuration project.
evals.assertion: Output includes verification checks after major operations
evals.pass_condition: true if deploy script or dashboard includes post-install verification commands with expected outcomes

evals.test_prompt:
/auto-admin-install-project
Context: Two PowerShell scripts for system monitoring.
evals.assertion: Step 2 outputs an improvement register with the specified columns
evals.pass_condition: true if output includes a table or structured list with: Item, Component, Impact, Effort, Recommended
